summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates environment, filesystem, and AWS credentials to hardcoded IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that collects the installer's environment variables, recursively reads files under /app, queries the AWS instance metadata service (169.254.169.254) via IMDSv2 to obtain IAM role credentials, and enumerates and dumps AWS Secrets Manager secrets in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. The collected data is chunked and PUT over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.80:30543 (with a 127.0.0.1:30543 fallback). Execution is gated by a hostname regex matching a 12-character hex string (Docker container ID) and a cwd containing /app/node_modules, indicating sandbox/CTF-style target selection. The package self-describes as a verdaccio supply-chain test payload and has no legitimate functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
